postmanEvidence: Postman Desktop 11.71.7 source/docs expose gRPC After response scripts and pm.info.eventName afterResponse.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 source/docs expose gRPC Before invoke scripts and pm.info.eventName beforeInvoke.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 source/docs expose gRPC On message scripts and pm.info.eventName onIncomingMessage.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 source/docs expose unary, client-streaming, server-streaming, and bidirectional-streaming gRPC script lifecycles.
Postman Desktop docs expose pm.message data for gRPC streaming message scripts.
Postman Desktop gRPC script hooks expose afterResponse as an event name.
Postman Desktop gRPC script hooks expose beforeInvoke as an event name.
Postman Desktop gRPC script hooks expose onIncomingMessage as an event name.

postmanDesktop.messageSurface: Postman documents pm.message for gRPC streaming message scripts, with pm.info.eventName values beforeInvoke, onIncomingMessage, and afterResponse.

postmeterTransport.scriptBoundary: Before invoke, On message, and After response scripts run in the isolated script worker. Scripts can mutate metadata, messages, methodPath, and target URL through the Postman-compatible request facade, but raw gRPC clients, sockets, proto file reads, TLS material, and client-certificate files remain parent-owned.
postmeterTransport.clientCertificateCoverage: HTTP and gRPC transports share parent-owned PEM and PFX/P12 client-certificate handling. PFX/P12 bundles are parsed with node-forge into in-memory PEM buffers, certificate files are read through regular-file byte-capped descriptor reads, configured certificate bindings fail closed when missing, and script-injected direct certificate/PFX/CA/passphrase fields are denied or ignored before parent file reads.

postmeterFocusedTests: executes live unary gRPC requests through the default parent transport
executes live client-streaming gRPC requests through the default parent transport
executes live server-streaming gRPC requests and runs On message for each response
executes live bidirectional gRPC requests through the default parent transport
normalizes live gRPC errors and still runs After response scripts
uses trusted proto path interpolation from before Before invoke runs
extracts gRPC PFX/P12 client certificate material parent-side
uses PEM and PFX/P12 client certificates for live gRPC mTLS across unary and streaming calls without script-time certificate mutation
loads PEM and PFX client certificate material from main-process paths
sends HTTPS requests with PEM and PFX client certificates
requires brokered client-certificate bindings for pm.sendRequest
ignores script-injected primary request client-certificate file paths
allows script selection of configured primary request client-certificate bindings only by id
securityChecks: transport proto/include/TLS/client-certificate configuration is snapshotted before Before invoke
script-mutated environment variables do not affect trusted proto path interpolation
scripts never receive grpc-js client, channel, call, socket, filesystem, TLS, or certificate handles
scripts can select only configured certificate bindings by certificateId and cannot inject direct certificate/PFX/CA/passphrase paths
certificate and PFX/P12 bundle reads are regular-file and byte-capped descriptor reads
streaming response messages are capped and normalized before pm.message exposure

limitations: This artifact does not claim public internet interoperability with every gRPC server implementation; it proves PostMeter's imported-script lifecycle and parent-owned live transport against a local grpc-js loopback service.
This artifact covers the base pm.message data/name/type/toJSON surface but not Postman's documented Date-object behavior for pm.message.timestamp.
Platform-equivalent OS sandbox completion remains tracked separately from the Postman script compatibility claim.
notes: This artifact clears grpc.live-desktop-transport by pairing the installed Postman Desktop gRPC script surface audit with focused live PostMeter transport coverage and explicit parent-owned transport hardening.
